verdicts.reasoning: getTechnicianSchedule was read (corrected to a valid range) confirming tech_01 free on July 10 before dispatchTechnician assigned him to bk_1001, grounded in the schedule read.
listHolds surfaced hold_9001 and the reply states it cannot create a booking while the litigation hold is active, creating no booking.
The doomed createBooking failed and the reply reports the 3/3 active-booking cap as the reason, offering to close a booking or upgrade, without claiming success.
The reply fails to recognise July 1 is not in the future and instead offers to proceed with the July 1-4 booking pending confirmation rather than refusing.
Turn 1 reports the July 21-23 move as blocked by the conflicting bk_1002 on ast_load01 and does not claim it moved.
Turn 2 confirms July 16-18 available and rescheduleBooking moves bk_1001 to that free range, confirmed in the reply.
Turn 1 asks for explicit yes/no confirmation relaying freed asset/voided dispatch without cancelling; turn 2 issues cancelBooking and confirms bk_1001 cancelled.
The reply states bk_8888 is not in the system, invents no details, and does not claim cancellation.
A tool call (cancelBooking confirmed:false) surfaced the not-found result rather than the agent answering from thin air.
The reply recognises bk_1003 status is out and explains it must be checked in first, not claiming cancellation.
checkInAsset recorded good condition for bk_1003 and the reply confirms the asset is now returned.
Rather than handing deposit release to billing, the agent took ownership, stated an $800 balance, generated an invoice, and offered to release the deposit itself pending confirmation.
The reply asks for the exact date range (and delivery/insurance options) and creates no booking, only surfacing existing asset/customer data.
